content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes where each node is numbered from 111 to NNN.

Suppose you root the tree at node RRR.

Then, the score of a permutation PPP of length NNN is defined as the **size** of the set SSS containing LCA(Pi,Pi+1)\texttt{LCA}(P_i, P_{i+1})LCA(Pi​,Pi+1​) for all 1≤i<N1\leq i \lt N1≤i<N.

Find the **minimum** score of a permutation over all possible roots and all possible permutations of length NNN.

You need to print the root RRR and the permutation PPP which results in the minimum score.

In case there are multiple answers, you may print any.

Note:


A permutation PPP of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.
LCA(A,B)\texttt{LCA}(A, B)LCA(A,B) denotes the lowest common ancestor of nodes AAA and BBB.

Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test contains an integer NNN, denoting the number of nodes in the tree.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines contain two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between the nodes uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.


Output Format
For each test case, print two lines.


In the first line, output RRR, the root for which a permutation has minimum score.

In the second line, print NNN space separated integers, denoting the permutation with minimum score.


If there are multiple answers, you can print any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider R=2R = 2R=2 and P=[1,2]P = [1, 2]P=[1,2]. Thus, S={LCA(1,2)}={2}S = \{\texttt{LCA}(1, 2)\} = \{2\}S={LCA(1,2)}={2}.
Thus, the score is ∣S∣=1|S| = 1∣S∣=1, which is minimum possible.
Test case 222: Consider R=3R = 3R=3 and P=[1,5,3,2,4]P = [1, 5, 3, 2, 4]P=[1,5,3,2,4]. Thus, S={LCA(1,5),LCA(5,3),LCA(3,2),LCA(2,4)}={3}S = \{\texttt{LCA}(1, 5), \texttt{LCA}(5, 3), \texttt{LCA}(3, 2), \texttt{LCA}(2, 4)\} = \{3\}S={LCA(1,5),LCA(5,3),LCA(3,2),LCA(2,4)}={3}.
Thus, the score is ∣S∣=1|S| = 1∣S∣=1, which is minimum possible.
